questions.text: The type of nutrition in which organisms make their own food is
questions.explanation: Autotrophs produce their own food, usually through photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which organ is affected by the disease called goiter?
questions.explanation: Goiter is an enlargement of the thyroid gland.

questions.text: The part of the cell that controls what enters and leaves is the
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is selectively permeable.

questions.text: In the human heart, the right atrium receives blood from the
questions.explanation: The right atrium receives deoxygenated blood from the body.

questions.text: Which of the following is a communicable disease?
questions.explanation: Tuberculosis is caused by bacteria and can spread.

questions.text: The process by which plants make food occurs in the
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis takes place in chloroplasts.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a mineral deficiency in plants?
questions.explanation: Mineral deficiencies cause various symptoms including chlorosis and stunted growth.

questions.text: The blood cells that fight infection are the
questions.explanation: White blood cells are part of the immune system.

questions.text: The organ that produces urine is the
questions.explanation: Kidneys produce urine.

questions.text: In genetics, the physical appearance of an organism is called
questions.explanation: Phenotype is the observable trait.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a characteristic of living things?
questions.explanation: Not all living things can move from place to place (e.g., plants).

questions.text: The basic structural unit of all living organisms is the
questions.explanation: The cell is the smallest unit of life.

questions.text: In humans, the respiratory organ is the
questions.explanation: Lungs are the organs of respiration.

questions.text: The pigment that makes plants green is
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs light for photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the kidney?
questions.explanation: Kidneys filter blood to remove waste.

questions.text: The type of blood vessel that returns blood to the heart is the
questions.explanation: Veins return deoxygenated blood to the heart.

questions.text: In plants, the male reproductive part is the
questions.explanation: The anther produces pollen.

questions.text: Which vitamin is needed for healthy bones and teeth?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D aids calcium absorption for bones.

questions.text: The process of cell eating is called
questions.explanation: Phagocytosis is the engulfing of large particles.

questions.text: The hormone that regulates sugar level in blood is
questions.explanation: Insulin lowers blood glucose levels.

questions.text: The process of taking in food and breaking it down is called
questions.explanation: Digestion is the mechanical and chemical breakdown of food.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of an arthropod?
questions.explanation: Cockroaches are insects, which are arthropods.

questions.text: The structure that stores genetic information is the
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains DNA.

questions.text: In the human heart, the chamber that pumps blood to the body is the
questions.explanation: The left ventricle pumps oxygenated blood to the body.

questions.text: Which of the following is a hereditary disease?
questions.explanation: Sickle cell anemia is an inherited genetic disorder.

questions.text: The part of the plant that anchors it in the soil is the
questions.explanation: Roots anchor plants and absorb water and minerals.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the skeletal system?
questions.explanation: Bone marrow produces blood cells.

questions.text: The gas produced by plants during respiration is
questions.explanation: CO2 is released during respiration.

questions.text: In the human eye, the part that focuses light is the
questions.explanation: The lens adjusts shape to focus light on the retina.

questions.text: The type of pollution that causes ozone layer depletion is
questions.explanation: CFCs and other pollutants deplete the ozone layer.

questions.text: The largest organ in the human body is the
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ by surface area.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of all living things?
questions.explanation: All living things reproduce to pass on genetic material.

questions.text: The type of cell division that results in two identical daughter cells is
questions.explanation: Mitosis produces two identical diploid cells.

questions.text: In plants, the tissue that transports food is the
questions.explanation: Phloem transports dissolved sugars.

questions.text: Which of the following is a vector-borne disease?
questions.explanation: Sleeping sickness is transmitted by tsetse flies.

questions.text: The part of the ear that contains the sensory cells for hearing is the
questions.explanation: The cochlea contains hair cells that detect sound.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the liver?
questions.explanation: The liver produces bile for digestion.

questions.text: The movement of molecules from low to high concentration requires
questions.explanation: Active transport requires energy to move against the concentration gradient.

questions.text: In genetics, a recessive gene will only show in the phenotype when
questions.explanation: Recessive traits require two copies to be expressed.

questions.text: The process by which plants lose water through leaves is called
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the loss of water vapor from plants.

questions.text: The type of reproduction that involves the fusion of male and female gametes is
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of gametes.

questions.text: Which of the following organisms is a decomposer?
questions.explanation: Mushrooms decompose dead organic matter.

questions.text: The blood vessels that carry blood away from the heart are called
questions.explanation: Arteries carry blood away from the heart.

questions.text: In plants, the opening and closing of stomata is controlled by
questions.explanation: Guard cells regulate stomatal aperture.

questions.text: Which vitamin is produced when the skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D synthesis occurs in the skin upon UV exposure.

questions.text: The organ responsible for filtering blood in the excretory system is the
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter blood to form urine.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a binomial nomenclature?
questions.explanation: Homo sapiens is the scientific name for humans.

questions.text: The process by which plants synthesize food is called
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis converts CO2 and H2O to glucose using light energy.

questions.text: Which of the following is a deficiency disease of iron?
questions.explanation: Iron deficiency leads to anemia.

questions.text: The site of gas exchange in the lungs is the
questions.explanation: Alveoli provide a large surface area for gas exchange.

questions.text: The study of cells is called
questions.explanation: Cytology is the study of cell structure and function.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a part of the human digestive system?
questions.explanation: The trachea is part of the respiratory system.

questions.text: The blood cells that carry oxygen are
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain hemoglobin which binds oxygen.

questions.text: In plants, the male gamete is contained in the
questions.explanation: The pollen grain contains male gametes.

questions.text: The process of losing leaves in plants is called
questions.explanation: Deciduous plants shed their leaves seasonally.

questions.text: Which organ is affected by the disease pneumonia?
questions.explanation: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the lungs.

questions.text: The type of cell division that produces gametes is
questions.explanation: Meiosis produces haploid gametes.

questions.text: Which of the following is a renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable.

questions.text: The part of the brain that controls breathing is the
questions.explanation: The medulla controls automatic functions like breathing.

questions.text: In genetics, the letters representing genes are usually written in
questions.explanation: Dominant alleles are represented by uppercase letters.

questions.text: The type of tissue that lines the body cavities is
questions.explanation: Epithelial tissue covers surfaces and lines cavities.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a drug abuse?
questions.explanation: Smoking is a harmful habit and form of drug abuse.

questions.text: The site of aerobic respiration in the cell is the
questions.explanation: The Krebs cycle and electron transport chain occur in mitochondria.

questions.text: In the human ear, the auditory nerve carries impulses to the
questions.explanation: The auditory nerve transmits signals to the brain for interpretation.

questions.text: Which of the following is an endocrine gland?
questions.explanation: The thyroid is an endocrine gland that releases hormones into the blood.

questions.text: The process by which plants grow towards water is called
questions.explanation: Hydrotropism is growth toward water.

questions.text: Which of the following is a result of air pollution?
questions.explanation: Air pollution causes acid rain, global warming, and smog.

questions.text: The type of nutrition exhibited by mushrooms is
questions.explanation: Mushrooms are heterotrophic fungi that decompose organic matter.

questions.text: The functional unit of the lung is the
questions.explanation: Alveoli are tiny air sacs where gas exchange occurs.

questions.text: In the nitrogen cycle, the process that converts ammonia to nitrates is
questions.explanation: Nitrifying bacteria convert ammonia to nitrates.

questions.text: The smallest unit of classification is the
questions.explanation: Species is the basic unit of classification.

questions.text: Which of the following is a sex-linked disease?
questions.explanation: Color blindness is an X-linked recessive disorder.

questions.text: The part of the brain responsible for balance is the
questions.explanation: The cerebellum coordinates movement and balance.

questions.text: In plants, the vascular tissue that transports water is the
questions.explanation: Xylem conducts water and minerals.

questions.text: The type of immunity where antibodies are transferred is called
questions.explanation: Passive immunity involves receiving antibodies from another source.

questions.text: Which blood group is the universal recipient?
questions.explanation: AB can receive blood from any type.

questions.text: The process of change from larva to adult is called
questions.explanation: Metamorphosis is the transformation from larval to adult form.

questions.text: In human genetics, if a child has blood group O, the parents must have
questions.explanation: To have type O, both parents must contribute an O allele.

questions.text: Which organ is affected by the disease hepatitis?
questions.explanation: Hepatitis is inflammation of the liver.

questions.text: The part of the plant that absorbs water is the
questions.explanation: Root hairs increase surface area for water absorption.

questions.text: Which of the following organisms is a consumer?
questions.explanation: Goats are herbivores (primary consumers) that eat producers.

questions.text: The process by which organisms maintain a stable internal environment is called
questions.explanation: Homeostasis is the regulation of internal conditions.

questions.text: Which of the following is a water-borne disease?
questions.explanation: Cholera is transmitted through contaminated water.

questions.text: In plants, the cuticle is found on the
questions.explanation: The cuticle is a waxy layer on leaf surfaces that prevents water loss.

questions.text: The human sex chromosomes in a female are
questions.explanation: Females have two X chromosomes.

questions.text: Which organelle is involved in the synthesis of fatty acids?
questions.explanation: Smooth ER synthesizes lipids.

questions.text: The reflex that protects the eye is the
questions.explanation: The blink reflex protects the eye from foreign objects.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of acquired immunity?
questions.explanation: Acquired immunity develops after exposure to pathogens.

questions.text: The type of fruit that develops from the ovary is called
questions.explanation: True fruits develop from the ovary.

questions.text: In the carbon cycle, carbon dioxide is returned to the atmosphere through
questions.explanation: Respiration and decomposition release CO2.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of monera?
questions.explanation: Monera are prokaryotes lacking a membrane-bound nucleus.

questions.text: The organ that pumps blood throughout the body is the
questions.explanation: The heart is the pump of the circulatory system.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the cytoskeleton?
questions.explanation: The cytoskeleton provides structural support and enables movement.

questions.text: In the human heart, the left ventricle has a thicker wall because it
questions.explanation: The left ventricle pumps blood to the entire body, requiring more force.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-specific defense mechanism?
questions.explanation: Phagocytosis is a non-specific defense.

questions.text: The site of the Calvin cycle in photosynthesis is the
questions.explanation: The Calvin cycle occurs in the stroma.

questions.text: The number of chromosomes in human somatic cells is
questions.explanation: Humans have 46 chromosomes (23 pairs) in diploid cells.

questions.text: Which of the following is a connective tissue?
questions.explanation: Blood is a connective tissue.

questions.text: The organ that stores urine before excretion is the
questions.explanation: The bladder stores urine.

questions.text: The process by which green plants make food is
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis converts light energy to chemical energy.

questions.text: Which of the following is the most abundant gas in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere.

questions.text: The site of protein synthesis in the cell is the
questions.explanation: Ribosomes synthesize proteins.

questions.text: The part of the human ear that amplifies sound is the
questions.explanation: The ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes) amplify and transmit sound.

questions.text: In plants, the transport of water is mainly by
questions.explanation: Transpiration pull drives water movement up xylem.

questions.text: Which of the following is a deficiency disease of protein?
questions.explanation: Kwashiorkor is caused by severe protein deficiency.

questions.text: The type of blood vessel that has valves is the
questions.explanation: Veins have valves to prevent backflow of blood.

questions.text: Which organ produces urea?
questions.explanation: The liver converts ammonia to urea.

questions.text: The process by which plants respond to gravity is called
questions.explanation: Geotropism is growth in response to gravity.

questions.text: In human genetics, a trait that appears in offspring is called
questions.explanation: A dominant trait expresses itself when present.

questions.text: The part of the flower that produces female gametes is the
questions.explanation: The ovule contains the egg cell (female gamete).

questions.text: The gas produced during anaerobic respiration in plants is
questions.explanation: Anaerobic respiration produces CO2 as a byproduct.

questions.text: The type of reproduction that involves only one parent is
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction involves one parent and produces genetically identical offspring.

questions.text: Which part of the brain controls heart rate?
questions.explanation: The medulla controls vital functions including heart rate.

questions.text: The function of the sebaceous gland is to
questions.explanation: Sebaceous glands produce sebum to lubricate skin and hair.

questions.text: In plants, the cells that guard stomata are called
questions.explanation: Guard cells regulate stomatal opening.

questions.text: Which of the following is a protozoan disease?
questions.explanation: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium, a protozoan.

questions.text: The part of the neuron that receives impulses is the
questions.explanation: Dendrites receive signals from other neurons.

questions.text: Which vitamin is produced by intestinal bacteria?
questions.explanation: Vitamin K is synthesized by gut bacteria.

questions.text: The hormone that causes the uterus to contract during labor is
questions.explanation: Oxytocin stimulates uterine contractions during childbirth.

questions.text: In a food web, the organisms that break down dead matter are
questions.explanation: Decomposers recycle nutrients from dead organic matter.

questions.text: The movement of water through the soil into the roots is called
questions.explanation: Imbibition is the uptake of water by dry seeds or soil particles.

questions.text: Which of the following organisms is a producer?
questions.explanation: Grass is a producer (autotroph) that makes its own food.

questions.text: The part of the brain that controls voluntary actions is the
questions.explanation: The cerebrum controls conscious thought and voluntary movements.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the large intestine?
questions.explanation: The large intestine absorbs water and electrolytes.

questions.text: The type of joint in the skull is called
questions.explanation: Skull bones are joined by immovable sutures.

questions.text: In the nitrogen cycle, bacteria that convert nitrates to nitrogen gas are
questions.explanation: Denitrifying bacteria convert nitrates to atmospheric nitrogen.

questions.text: Which of the following is a symptom of diabetes mellitus?
questions.explanation: Polyuria (excessive urination) is a symptom of diabetes.

questions.text: The eye defect where distant objects appear blurry is
questions.explanation: Myopia (nearsightedness) causes distant objects to be blurry.

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for lipid synthesis?
questions.explanation: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum synthesizes lipids.

questions.text: The process of sperms being produced is called
questions.explanation: Spermatogenesis is the production of sperm cells.

questions.text: Which of the following is a type of permanent tissue in plants?
questions.explanation: Sclerenchyma is a supporting permanent tissue with thickened walls.

questions.text: The organ that removes carbon dioxide from the blood is the
questions.explanation: Lungs remove CO2 through exhalation.

questions.text: In genetics, the alternative forms of a gene are called
questions.explanation: Alleles are different versions of a gene.

questions.text: Which structure prevents food from entering the trachea during swallowing?
questions.explanation: The epiglottis covers the trachea during swallowing.

questions.text: The type of tooth used for grinding food is the
questions.explanation: Molars have broad surfaces for grinding.

questions.text: Which of the following is a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: CO2 traps heat in the atmosphere.

questions.text: The functional unit of the kidney is the
questions.explanation: Nephrons filter blood to produce urine.

questions.text: In plants, wilting occurs when
questions.explanation: Wilting results from loss of turgor pressure due to water deficit.

questions.text: Which blood cell is involved in immune response?
questions.explanation: White blood cells fight infection.

questions.text: The part of the flower that develops into the fruit is the
questions.explanation: The ovary develops into the fruit.

questions.text: The structure that connects the mother to the fetus is the
questions.explanation: The umbilical cord connects the fetus to the placenta.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the skin?
questions.explanation: Skin produces vitamin D when exposed to sunlight.

questions.text: In plants, the transport of food materials is through the
questions.explanation: Phloem transports dissolved sugars from leaves to other parts.

questions.text: The part of the brain that controls body temperature is the
questions.explanation: The hypothalamus regulates body temperature.

questions.text: Which organelle is involved in cellular waste disposal?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain enzymes that break down waste materials.

questions.text: The deficiency of iodine causes
questions.explanation: Iodine deficiency leads to goiter (thyroid enlargement).

questions.text: Which blood vessel carries blood away from the heart?
questions.explanation: Arteries carry blood away from the heart.

questions.text: In the human eye, the blind spot is where the
questions.explanation: The optic nerve exits at the optic disc, which lacks photoreceptors.

questions.text: The process by which plants lose their leaves is called
questions.explanation: Abscission is the shedding of leaves.

questions.text: Which of the following is a sexually transmitted infection?
questions.explanation: Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a function of the cell membrane?
questions.explanation: Protein synthesis occurs on ribosomes, not the cell membrane.

questions.text: The process of conversion of glucose to ethanol and carbon dioxide is called
questions.explanation: Fermentation in yeast produces ethanol and CO2.

questions.text: Which organ produces bile?
questions.explanation: The liver produces bile, which is stored in the gallbladder.

questions.text: In plants, the male gametes are produced in the
questions.explanation: The anther produces pollen containing male gametes.

questions.text: Which vitamin is stored in the liver?
questions.explanation: Vitamin A is stored in the liver.

questions.text: The smallest bone in the human body is the
questions.explanation: The stapes in the middle ear is the smallest bone.

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll for photosynthesis.

questions.text: The type of reproduction that produces genetically identical offspring is
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction produces clones.

questions.text: In humans, the period of pregnancy is approximately
questions.explanation: Normal human gestation is about 40 weeks.

questions.text: Which of the following is a macronutrient required by plants?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is needed in large quantities for plant growth.

questions.text: The process of intake of solid particles by cells is called
questions.explanation: Phagocytosis is the engulfing of large particles by cells.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of Kingdom Plantae?
questions.explanation: Plants are autotrophic, producing their own food through photosynthesis.

questions.text: The hormone that prepares the body for emergency situations is
questions.explanation: Adrenaline (epinephrine) triggers the fight-or-flight response.

questions.text: In the human digestive system, protein digestion begins in the
questions.explanation: Pepsin in the stomach begins breaking down proteins.

questions.text: Which structure in the cell contains genetic material?
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains DNA, the genetic material.

questions.text: The part of the flower that produces pollen grains is the
questions.explanation: The anther produces microspores that develop into pollen grains.

questions.text: Which blood cells are involved in blood clotting?
questions.explanation: Platelets are essential for blood clot formation.

questions.text: The movement of water across a semi-permeable membrane is called
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the movement of water from low to high solute concentration.

questions.text: Which organ is the main organ of the excretory system?
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter blood and produce urine.

questions.text: The ability of the body to resist infection is called
questions.explanation: Immunity is the body's ability to fight pathogens.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a component of the human circulatory system?
questions.explanation: The lungs are part of the respiratory system, not the circulatory system.

questions.text: The cell theory states that all living things are composed of
questions.explanation: Cell theory states that all organisms are made of cells.

questions.text: Which gas is absorbed by plants from the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb CO2 for use in photosynthesis.

questions.text: The male hormone responsible for secondary sexual characteristics is
questions.explanation: Testosterone is the male sex hormone that promotes male secondary characteristics.

questions.text: Which organelle is found in both plant and animal cells?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are present in both plant and animal cells.

questions.text: The process by which plants respond to light is called
questions.explanation: Phototropism is the growth response of plants toward light.

questions.text: In the human heart, the right ventricle pumps blood to the
questions.explanation: The right ventricle pumps deoxygenated blood to the lungs.

questions.text: Which of the following is a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Polio is caused by poliovirus.

questions.text: The part of the ear responsible for balance is the
questions.explanation: The semicircular canals detect head movement and help maintain balance.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-communicable disease?
questions.explanation: Diabetes is not transmitted from person to person.

questions.text: The movement of molecules from a region of high concentration to low concentration is called
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the passive movement of molecules from high to low concentration.

questions.text: Which organelle is the site of protein packaging?
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, packages, and sorts proteins for secretion.

questions.text: The type of nutrition in which organisms obtain food from other living organisms is
questions.explanation: Heterotrophs obtain food by consuming other organisms.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of the skeleton?
questions.explanation: Bone marrow produces red and white blood cells.

questions.text: The eye lens focuses light on the
questions.explanation: The lens focuses light rays onto the retina where photoreceptors detect light.

questions.text: In plants, water is transported through the
questions.explanation: Xylem transports water and minerals from roots to leaves.

questions.text: Which vitamin is needed for blood clotting?
questions.explanation: Vitamin K is essential for the synthesis of clotting factors.

questions.text: The genetic material in cells is
questions.explanation: DNA carries genetic information that controls cell activities.

questions.text: Which part of the brain is responsible for memory?
questions.explanation: The cerebrum, particularly the hippocampus, is involved in memory formation.

questions.text: The process by which plants lose water vapor is called
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the loss of water vapor from plant surfaces, mainly through stomata.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a function of the liver?
questions.explanation: Insulin is produced by the pancreas, not the liver.

questions.text: The fluid-filled space in a plant cell is the
questions.explanation: The central vacuole stores water, nutrients, and waste products in plant cells.

questions.text: Which blood group is the universal donor?
questions.explanation: Type O negative can be given to anyone because it lacks A, B, and Rh antigens.

questions.text: The primary function of the placenta is to

choices.C: Exchange materials between mother and fetus

questions.explanation: The placenta allows exchange of oxygen, nutrients, and waste between maternal and fetal blood.

questions.text: In which organ does most digestion occur?
questions.explanation: The small intestine is where most chemical digestion and nutrient absorption occurs.

questions.text: Which gas is released during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is released as a byproduct of photosynthesis.

questions.text: The process of cell division that results in four daughter cells is
questions.explanation: Meiosis produces four haploid daughter cells from one diploid cell.

questions.text: Which of the following is a function of white blood cells?
questions.explanation: White blood cells are part of the immune system and protect against pathogens.

questions.text: The dark reaction of photosynthesis takes place in the
questions.explanation: The Calvin cycle (dark reactions) occurs in the stroma of chloroplasts.

questions.text: The scientific name for man is
questions.explanation: Homo sapiens is the scientific name for modern humans.

questions.text: The energy currency of the cell is
questions.explanation: ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) is the primary energy currency used by cells.

questions.text: Which of the following is a decomposer?
questions.explanation: Mushrooms are fungi that decompose dead organic matter and recycle nutrients.

questions.text: The part of the eye that controls the amount of light entering is the
questions.explanation: The iris contains muscles that adjust pupil size to control light entry.

questions.text: In plants, the male reproductive part is the
questions.explanation: The anther produces pollen grains containing male gametes.

questions.text: Which of the following is a deficiency disease caused by lack of vitamin C?
questions.explanation: Scurvy is caused by deficiency of vitamin C and leads to bleeding gums and poor wound healing.

questions.text: The smallest blood vessels in the body are the
questions.explanation: Capillaries have thin walls allowing exchange of substances between blood and tissues.

questions.text: Which organelle contains digestive enzymes?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes for breaking down waste materials and cellular debris.

questions.text: The process of conversion of glucose to pyruvate is called
questions.explanation: Glycolysis is the breakdown of glucose to pyruvate, occurring in the cytoplasm.

questions.text: In humans, the sex chromosomes are
questions.explanation: Females are XX and males are XY in humans.

questions.text: Which part of the brain controls breathing and heart rate?
questions.explanation: The medulla oblongata controls vital autonomic functions including breathing and heart rate.

questions.text: Which of the following structures is found in plant cells but not in animal cells?
questions.explanation: Plant cells have a cell wall made of cellulose, which is absent in animal cells.

questions.text: The hormone that regulates blood sugar level in humans is
questions.explanation: Insulin is produced by the pancreas and helps cells absorb glucose from the blood.

questions.text: Which of the following is a communicable disease?
questions.explanation: Chickenpox is caused by a virus and can be transmitted from one person to another.

questions.text: The minute openings on the underside of leaves are called
questions.explanation: Stomata allow gas exchange (CO2 in, O2 out) and water vapor to exit the leaf.

questions.text: In human reproduction, the site of fertilization is the
questions.explanation: Fertilization normally occurs in the fallopian tube (oviduct).

questions.text: Which blood cells are responsible for fighting infections?
questions.explanation: White blood cells (leukocytes) are part of the immune system and fight pathogens.

questions.text: The process of loss of water in plants is called
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the loss of water vapor from the aerial parts of plants, mainly through stomata.

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the sites of protein synthesis where amino acids are assembled into polypeptide chains.

questions.text: The type of joint found in the knee is the
questions.explanation: The knee is a hinge joint that allows movement in one plane (bending and straightening).

questions.text: In genetics, the physical appearance of an organism is its
questions.explanation: Phenotype refers to the observable physical characteristics resulting from genotype and environment.

questions.text: The nucleus of a cell controls all cellular activities except
questions.explanation: Respiration occurs in the mitochondria and does not require nuclear control.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a function of the skeleton?
questions.explanation: The skeleton provides support, protection, and produces blood cells in marrow, but does not function in reproduction.

questions.text: The green pigment in plants that absorbs light for photosynthesis is
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs light energy, primarily red and blue wavelengths, for photosynthesis.

questions.text: In which part of the human ear is sound waves transduced into nerve impulses?
questions.explanation: The cochlea contains hair cells that convert sound vibrations into electrical nerve impulses.

questions.text: Which type of reproduction involves the fusion of gametes?
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of male and female gametes.

questions.text: The process by which water moves across a selectively permeable membrane is
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the movement of water molecules from an area of lower solute concentration to higher concentration.

questions.text: Which organ is responsible for filtering blood in the excretory system?
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter blood to remove waste products and regulate water and electrolyte balance.

questions.text: The gap between two neurons is called the
questions.explanation: The synapse is the small gap between neurons where neurotransmitters are released.

questions.text: Which vitamin is produced when the skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin when exposed to UV radiation from sunlight.

questions.text: In a food chain, the organisms that convert solar energy to chemical energy are
questions.explanation: Producers (plants) convert solar energy to chemical energy through photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of all living organisms?
questions.explanation: All living organisms must be able to reproduce to pass on genetic material to offspring.

questions.text: The basic unit of life is the
questions.explanation: The cell is the smallest unit that can carry out all life processes and is the basic structural unit of all living things.

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for cellular respiration?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell where ATP is produced through cellular respiration.

questions.text: In plants, gaseous exchange occurs through the
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores mainly found on the underside of leaves where gas exchange takes place.

questions.text: The blood vessel that carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs is the
questions.explanation: The pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood from the right ventricle to the lungs for oxygenation.

questions.text: Which of the following is a vector-borne disease?
questions.explanation: Malaria is transmitted by the female Anopheles mosquito vector.

questions.text: The process by which plants make food is called
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy (glucose).

questions.text: In the human digestive system, bile is produced by the
questions.explanation: Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder before being released into the duodenum.

questions.text: Which of the following is a hereditary disease?
questions.explanation: Sickle cell anemia is a genetic disorder caused by a mutation in the hemoglobin gene.

questions.text: The part of the brain that controls balance and coordination is the
questions.explanation: The cerebellum is responsible for coordinating voluntary movements and maintaining balance.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a compound?
questions.explanation: Oxygen (O2) is an element, not a compound.

questions.text: The mass number of an atom is the sum of
questions.explanation: Mass number = protons + neutrons.

questions.text: Which of the following gases is lighter than air?
questions.explanation: H2 (molar mass 2) is lighter than air (≈29).

questions.text: The process of converting fats to soap is called
questions.explanation: Saponification is the hydrolysis of fats with alkali.

questions.text: Which of the following is a Lewis acid?
questions.explanation: BF3 accepts an electron pair (Lewis acid).

questions.text: The colour of methyl orange in acidic medium is
questions.explanation: Methyl orange turns red in acid.

questions.text: Which of the following is a property of noble gases?
questions.explanation: Noble gases are generally colorless and inert.

questions.text: The reaction between an acid and a base is called
questions.explanation: Acid + Base → Salt + Water (Neutralization).

questions.text: Which of the following is a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: Methane (CH4) is a potent greenhouse gas.

questions.text: The systematic name of KClO3 is
questions.explanation: KClO3 is potassium chlorate.

questions.text: Which of the following is an allotrope of carbon?
questions.explanation: Diamond, graphite, and fullerene are allotropes of carbon.

questions.text: The number of electrons in the outer shell of oxygen is
questions.explanation: Oxygen has atomic number 8, so it has 6 valence electrons.

questions.text: Which of the following is a chemical property of metals?
questions.explanation: Reactivity with acids is a chemical property.

questions.text: The main constituent of natural gas is
questions.explanation: Methane (CH4) is the primary component of natural gas.

questions.text: Which of the following indicators is used for titration?
questions.explanation: All these indicators can be used in titrations.

questions.text: The oxidation state of iron in Fe2O3 is
Which of the following is a catalyst in the Haber process?
questions.explanation: Iron is the catalyst used in the Haber process.

questions.text: The type of bond in N2 molecule is
questions.explanation: N2 has a triple bond (N≡N).

questions.text: Which of the following is a soapless soap?
questions.explanation: Sodium lauryl sulfate is a synthetic detergent.

questions.text: The empirical formula of C6H12O6 is
questions.explanation: Dividing by 6 gives CH2O.

questions.text: Which of the following is a physical change?
questions.explanation: Dissolving sugar in water is a physical change as no new substance is formed.

questions.text: The maximum number of electrons in the second shell is
questions.explanation: The second shell can hold up to 8 electrons (2n² where n=2).

questions.text: Which of the following compounds is ionic?
questions.explanation: NaCl is an ionic compound formed from Na+ and Cl- ions.

questions.text: The general formula for alkenes is
questions.explanation: Alkenes have one double bond, giving the formula CnH2n.

questions.text: Which of the following is used to test for starch?
questions.explanation: Iodine turns blue-black in the presence of starch.

questions.text: The gas evolved when an acid reacts with a metal is
questions.explanation: Acids react with metals to produce hydrogen gas.

questions.text: Which of the following is a property of metals?
questions.explanation: Metals are malleable and ductile.

questions.text: The process of electrolysis is used to extract
questions.explanation: Electrolysis is used to extract both iron and sodium.

questions.text: Which of the following is a carbonate ore?
questions.explanation: Calamine (ZnCO3) is a carbonate ore.

questions.text: The bond in HCl molecule is
questions.explanation: HCl has a covalent bond as electrons are shared.

questions.text: Which of the following is a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Rusting of iron involves the formation of a new substance (Fe2O3), which is a chemical change.

questions.text: The atomic number of an element is the number of
questions.explanation: Atomic number equals the number of protons in the nucleus.

questions.text: Which of the following gases is responsible for acid rain?
questions.explanation: SO2 dissolves in rain water to form acid rain.

questions.text: The process of removing hardness of water is called
questions.explanation: Water softening removes Ca2+ and Mg2+ ions that cause hardness.

questions.text: Which of the following is a weak acid?
questions.explanation: Acetic acid (CH3COOH) partially dissociates in water, making it a weak acid.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of CH3CH2CH2OH is
questions.explanation: The correct IUPAC name is propan-1-ol.

questions.text: Which of the following elements is a noble gas?
questions.explanation: Helium is a noble gas with a complete outer electron shell.

questions.text: The rate of diffusion of gases is inversely proportional to
questions.explanation: Graham's law states rate ∝ 1/√M.

questions.text: Which of the following is a reducing agent?
questions.explanation: H2S donates electrons (reduces other substances).

questions.text: The pH of a neutral solution is
questions.explanation: pH 7 indicates a neutral solution at 25°C.
